title: callitris columellaris subsp. campestris
intro: Callitris columellaris subsp. campestris, also known as the grass pine or coastal cypress pine, is a subspecies of coniferous tree native to the southwestern coast of Western Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Callitris columellaris subsp. campestris include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable resource for the treatment of various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves and resin of this plant are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
